nodes.title: DAOD 6000 - Table of Contents
nodes.summary: The partial document covers policies and standards related to Information Management and Information Technology within the Canadian National Defence Department. It specifically details the contents of DAOD 6000-0, including sections such as Introduction, Definitions, Policy Direction, Consequences, Authorities, and References. nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: The partial document describes a Defense Administrative Order Directive (DAOD) related to Information Management and Information Technology within the Department of National Defence (DND) and Canadian Armed Forces (CAF). It outlines the scope of application, superseding directives, approval authority, and contact information. The document also provides definitions for key terms such as communications security, defence terminology, information management, information security, and information technology security.

nodes.title: Policy Direction
nodes.summary: The partial document covers the operational security standard for managing information technology security, including policy direction, context, and requirements for the Department of National Defence and the Canadian Armed Forces. It outlines the responsibilities of the Defence Chief Information Officer and the strategic context for managing information and technology, emphasizing collaboration and integrated business planning.

nodes.title: Authorities
nodes.summary: The partial document covers the establishment of strategic direction and governance structures for Information Management (IM) and Information Technology (IT) across the Department of National Defence (DND) and the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF). It includes ensuring the Defence Chief Information Officer (CIO) has visibility on all IM and IT activities, providing clear direction on the management of IM, IT, IT security, information security, COMSEC, and defence terminology, and outlining the authorities responsible for these tasks. The document also specifies the consequences of not implementing the requirements properly and provides references for related policies and directives.
